title: Task
description: An atomic unit of work within a problem pack. A task must have a binary done condition — it either holds or it does not. Tasks that cannot fail have not been scoped.

id.description: Unique task identifier in kebab-case. Referenced in agent submissions and issue titles. Example: source-inventory

problem_id.description: The problem pack this task belongs to. Must match the id field in problem.json. Example: climate-health/malaria-early-warning-africa

title.description: One sentence, action-oriented. The title should make clear what is being done and to what. Example: 'Inventory malaria and climate data sources for Sub-Saharan Africa'

status.description: Current workflow state. Only tasks with status 'scoped' are ready for contributors to pick up.

owner_role.description: The agent role guide best suited for this task. Contributors should read that guide before starting. See agents/ directory.

outcome.description: What exists when this task is complete. State the artifact or decision, not the effort. Example: 'A dated source inventory separating usable, limited, and rejected sources with explicit reasons for each classification.'

inputs.description: Specific data sources, prior artifacts, or documents needed to complete this task. Name them precisely enough that a contributor unfamiliar with the pack can find them.

expected_artifact.description: The specific file or section that changes when this task is done. Example: 'datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source'

validation_method.description: How a reviewer or replicator checks that the done condition holds. Must produce a verifiable outcome — not 'reviewer confirms the analysis is correct.' Example: 'Reviewer checks each source for URL, date, access status, and stated limitations.'

reviewer_needed.description: Role of reviewer required. Use one of: domain-reviewer, replicator, red-team-reviewer, field-reality-reviewer. Example: 'domain-reviewer'

safety_risk.description: Risk level for this task's output. low = framing, literature summaries. medium = data cleaning, ranking, models. high = operational recommendations, forecasts, resource allocation.

done_condition.description: A single falsifiable statement that holds when the task is complete. If the done condition is always reachable, the task has not been scoped. Example: 'At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons.'
